text.title: ACME TLS Renewal Automation Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: certbot in /etc/cron.weekly silently exits 1 for 90 days; cert expires; production 502 storm.
antipattern.root-cause: Cron + no log scraping = silent failure; no second-source probe of the live listener.
antipattern.fix: Replace with systemd timer + OnFailure unit that pages; add blackbox_exporter probe of the public endpoint with 14-day expiry alert.

antipattern.symptom: Cert file renewed on disk; nginx keeps serving the old cert (cached in worker memory); expiry triggers an outage.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote a renewal cron without a `--deploy-hook` to reload the listener.
antipattern.fix: Add `--deploy-hook 'systemctl reload nginx'`; verify with `openssl s_client -connect host:443 < /dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates` post-renewal.

antipattern.symptom: First-time issuance run against Let's Encrypt prod with a bad DNS challenge; account locked out of the domain for 7 days.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped staging assuming the config was right.
antipattern.fix: Always run against `--server https://acme-staging-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory` first; switch to prod only after the staging cert is verified.
